summary: Compose one ShellPage inside ShellApp's content region with a slotted BarTitle and application-owned routed content.
useWhen: A standard authenticated route needs shared page title chrome, sticky scroll compaction, and consistent content inset.
The page column can change width as global navigation or tools open and close.
avoidWhen: The experience does not use the standard authenticated ShellApp layout.
The content belongs to a drawer, modal, card, or fullscreen tool pane.
A ShellPage already owns the route; never nest ShellPages.

components.role: Owns global chrome and the single scrolling content region.
Owns the page main landmark, sticky header orchestration, responsive variants, and content inset.
Provides the page h1, inline route-section selector, Back intent, and page actions.

stateOwnership: ShellApp owns global navigation, tools, and scrolling; ShellPage never creates another scroll container.
ShellPage owns automatic BarTitle variant selection. Consumers do not synchronize BarTitle.variant when headerPresentation is auto.
The route owner owns heading copy, Back consequences, selected section value, available actions, data, forms, and product outcomes.
Keep dsSectionChange controlled by updating the route and passing the resolved value back to BarTitle.
accessibility: Render one active ShellPage so the application contains one routed-page main landmark.
Provide one BarTitle in slot=header and one concise heading so the page contains one h1.
Use destination-specific Back labels and preserve action labels across inline and overflow presentations.
Keep route loading, error, empty, success, and validation communication in the ShellPage content slot.
responsiveBehavior: Resolve viewport and persistent shell chrome into ShellPage.headerCapacity; ShellPage uses that explicit capacity plus scroll position to select expanded, compact, or constrained presentation.
Set primaryAction.collapse to never only when an ordinary routed-page action must preserve visible hierarchy; create and edit workflows use BarWorkflow instead.
Use contentInset none only for deliberately full-bleed products such as maps and canvases.
Place TabGroup inside ShellPage content for inner-page panels; route-level sections remain in BarTitle.

recipes.title: Routed page shell
recipes.summary: A standard page with an inline section selector and application-owned content.

files.content:
<ds-shell-app>
  <ds-shell-page header-capacity="roomy">
    <ds-bar-title
      id="drivers-header"
      slot="header"
      heading="Drivers"
      value="active"
    ></ds-bar-title>
    <section aria-label="Driver results">Page content</section>
  </ds-shell-page>
</ds-shell-app>

notes: The complete application also supplies PanelNav, BarNav, and PanelTools through ShellApp's named slots.
Do not put page content inside BarTitle.

recipes.title: React routed page shell
recipes.summary: Generated React wrappers with controlled route-section state.
notes: Resolve value from router state in production rather than keeping duplicate local route state.
Use contentInset="none" only for full-bleed pages.

recipes.title: Angular routed page shell
recipes.summary: Generated standalone adapters with router-owned page content.
notes: ShellApp belongs in the persistent application layout; routed page components render ShellPage in its default content slot.
Do not add an overflow-auto wrapper around ds-shell-page.
